\boxed{A}
**Clinical Reasoning:** The evidence indicates that adenoviruses are commonly used as viral vectors in gene therapies and vaccines, such as in the case of Ad26.COV2.S (J&J Covid-19 vaccine) and Ad26.ZEBOV (Ebola vaccine). These vectors utilize adenovirus capsids to deliver genetic material. Although other vectors like AAV are mentioned, the presence of explicit references to adenovirus-based delivery systems confirms that pRETRO-SUPER—being named after a retroviral-like system—is likely referring to a product derived from adenoviral platforms given contextual alignment with current vector technologies described in the literature. Therefore, despite "retro" in the name possibly suggesting retroviruses, real-world usage supports adenoviral classification based on documented applications.
